cpSummary: Overwhelmingly parents (mostly mothers) treating school-age daughters with long/thick hair after back-to-school lice outbreaks. Buyers are stressed, budget-conscious, and have usually tried 2-3 failed products (pharmacy or natural remedies) before landing on the winner.

usageScenarios.label: Back-to-school infestation (maternelle/primaire)
usageScenarios.reason: Most reviews mention lice caught at school, often in September or after holidays

usageScenarios.label: Repeat infestation after failed pharmacy products
usageScenarios.reason: Buyers arrive having burned through 2-3 other brands first

usageScenarios.label: Whole-family treatment (mother + siblings)
usageScenarios.reason: One bottle rarely enough — parents treat themselves and all children simultaneously

usageScenarios.label: Long/thick/curly/afro hair challenge
usageScenarios.reason: Texture of hair drives product choice and quantity needed

usageScenarios.label: Preventive application
usageScenarios.reason: A subset use it proactively when outbreak announced at school

csSummary: Two sharply divided camps: enthusiasts who call it 'miraculous in one application' and frustrated buyers who report live lice after 8-12 hours of exposure — suggesting real variability in hair-coverage technique, resistance, and expectation management around nits vs lice.

negativeTopics.label: Product ineffective — live lice after treatment
negativeTopics.reason: Dominant 1-2★ complaint: lice still alive after full exposure time
bullets: Live lice found with comb after 8-12h overnight pose
Entire family retreated with zero result
quotes: "After 12h of exposure, lice are still alive — first time this has ever happened with an anti-lice treatment."
"I've done 3 treatments in a week — still finding live lice. 15 euros in the bin."

negativeTopics.label: Nits (lentes) survive — requires 2nd application
negativeTopics.reason: Even positive reviewers note a second round 7 days later is needed
bullets: Product kills adults but nits hatch days later
'One application' marketing claim contradicted by reality
quotes: "The lice die but the nits are another story — they come back a week later."
"One application is not enough despite what the box says."

negativeTopics.label: Cheap plastic comb — inadequate
negativeTopics.reason: Universal gripe: the included comb is flimsy, teeth too short/wide, breaks easily
bullets: Plastic comb breaks on first use
Teeth too short for long/thick hair
Users advise buying separate metal comb
quotes: "The comb is plastic, fragile, better to invest in a metal one."
"Comb broke at the first use — useless on thick hair."

negativeTopics.label: Too greasy — hard to rinse out
negativeTopics.reason: Silicone/oil base requires 2-3 shampoos to remove, leaves hair oily
quotes: "Product is like oil, I had to do 3 shampoos to get it out."
"Very greasy, protect floor and clothes during application."

negativeTopics.label: Irritation — burning eyes, scalp sensitivity
negativeTopics.reason: Spray drift into eyes, scalp reactions reported
quotes: "The product literally burned my son's eyes — he screamed."
"Scalp sensitive for a week after, even without lice."

negativeTopics.label: Too expensive / small bottle
negativeTopics.reason: Price perceived as high relative to pharmacy, bottle too small for families
bullets: 150ml not enough for long hair × multiple family members
quotes: "150ml is tiny when you have long hair and a whole family to treat."
"A real budget when you have kids in nursery school."

negativeTopics.label: Damaged / opened on arrival
negativeTopics.reason: Multiple reports of broken bottles, missing combs, or crystals clogging nozzle
bullets: Crystals formed in lotion block nozzle
quotes: "Bottle arrived open, product spilled everywhere."
"Full of white crystals, impossible to use properly."

negativeTopics.label: Overnight application too constraining
negativeTopics.reason: Some formats require 8+ hour overnight pose — hard on young kids
bullets: Kids can't sit still overnight with product
Rinsing in morning disrupts school routine
quotes: "Leaving it on a child's head for 8 hours is really constraining."
"Impossible to make a 3-year-old keep it on all night."

negativeInsights.finding: Marketing promises 'effective in one application / 5 minutes' but nits require 7-10 days to hatch and second treatment.
negativeInsights.implication: Expectation mismatch drives the majority of 1-2★ reviews — buyers feel deceived even when the product partially worked.

negativeInsights.finding: Included plastic comb is universally rated inferior to metal combs.
negativeInsights.implication: Every brand that bundles a plastic comb gets the same complaint — opportunity to differentiate with proper stainless steel comb.

negativeInsights.finding: Silicone/oil spray drift causes significant eye irritation, especially on young children.
negativeInsights.implication: Safety-label failure risk; claims of 'suitable from 6 months' contradicted by painful user reports.

negativeInsights.finding: 150ml bottles insufficient for multi-child families with long hair.
negativeInsights.implication: Family-size (400-500ml) SKU with better price-per-ml would capture repeat buyers.

negativeInsights.finding: 'Satisfait ou rembourse' 3-day refund window is seen as fraudulent — shorter than the treatment cycle.
negativeInsights.implication: Refund policy shorter than treatment cycle generates repeat 1★ reviews citing bad faith.

negativeInsights.finding: Puressentiel and natural-formula products under-deliver on heavy infestations.
negativeInsights.implication: Buyers who started natural end up returning to dimethicone-based products — positioning the natural product as 'prevention only' would set correct expectations.

positiveTopics.label: Effective in one application
positiveTopics.reason: Dominant 5★ theme: kills lice and nits in single overnight or 5-min application
bullets: Dead lice visible on comb right after rinsing
Saves weeks of fighting with other products
quotes: "One application and goodbye lice and nits — I recommend 100%."
"After months of fighting, this product saved us in one use."

positiveTopics.label: Best after trying many alternatives
positiveTopics.reason: Users explicitly compare against 3-5 other failed products from pharmacy
bullets: Finally found 'the one' after long battle
quotes: "After trying Marie Rose, Paranix, Apaisyl, essential oils — this is the only one that worked."
"The only truly effective anti-lice product I've found."

positiveTopics.label: Easy spray application
positiveTopics.reason: Spray nozzle praised as faster/cleaner than bottle-pour lotions
bullets: No waste compared to bottle formats
Quick to apply on squirming kids
quotes: "Much easier than bottle-format products — just pchit and done."
"Spray lets you dose precisely without waste."

positiveTopics.label: Short 5-minute exposure time
positiveTopics.reason: Compared favorably to competitors requiring 15-30min or overnight
bullets: No need to sleep with product
quotes: "5 min is top — works great, I recommend!"
"Much easier than overnight products for young kids."

positiveTopics.label: Cheaper than pharmacy
positiveTopics.reason: Amazon price routinely cited as half the pharmacy price
quotes: "100% effective and half the price of pharmacy."
"Hard to find in pharmacy, so grateful Amazon sells it."

positiveTopics.label: No harsh chemicals / family-safe
positiveTopics.reason: Dimethicone or essential-oil formulas praised as gentle for kids from 6 months
bullets: Suitable for toddlers and pregnant women
quotes: "Formulated without neurotoxic insecticide, acts mechanically."
"Suitable for kids from 6 months and adults — finally a safe option."

positiveTopics.label: Pleasant / neutral smell
positiveTopics.reason: Unlike vinegar or older-generation products, no chemical stench
quotes: "No unpleasant smell, kids didn't complain."
"Smells fine, not the chemical horror of other brands."

positiveTopics.label: Hair stays healthy after treatment
positiveTopics.reason: Oil base doubles as conditioner, hair soft after rinsing
quotes: "Hair is silky after, like a conditioner."
"Doesn't damage hair, good for long afro-textured hair."

positiveInsights.finding: Biggest advocates are repeat-customer parents who bought after 3-5 failed attempts with other brands.
positiveInsights.implication: Marketing angle: 'the last anti-lice product you'll ever buy' — position as graduation from failed pharmacy alternatives.

positiveInsights.finding: 5-minute pose time is the #1 differentiator called out vs overnight competitors.
positiveInsights.implication: Fast-pose format commands premium and drives 5★ reviews from stressed parents.

positiveInsights.finding: Dimethicone formulas consistently praised for physical (non-insecticide) action.
positiveInsights.implication: 'No pesticides / safe for 6m+' is a durable positioning hook as buyers grow anti-chemical.

positiveInsights.finding: Amazon price consistently 40-50% below pharmacy — key purchase driver.
positiveInsights.implication: Losing Amazon channel would collapse the category; pharmacy brands must decide whether to own DTC or cede to e-commerce.

positiveInsights.finding: Hair-conditioning side-effect (softness, detangling) appears as unexpected delight.
positiveInsights.implication: Lean into 'treatment + hair care' dual-benefit in the packaging copy.

buyersMotivation.label: Urgent eradication of active infestation
buyersMotivation.reason: Most buyers arrive in panic mode after discovering lice

buyersMotivation.label: Frustration with failed previous products
buyersMotivation.reason: Having wasted money on 2-3 brands, searching for 'the one that works'

buyersMotivation.label: Safe for young children / no chemicals
buyersMotivation.reason: Toddlers, babies, sensitive scalps drive formula preference

buyersMotivation.label: Cheaper than pharmacy alternative
buyersMotivation.reason: Budget pressure when treating multiple family members

customerExpectations.label: Kill 100% of lice AND nits in one application
customerExpectations.reason: Packaging promises drive this expectation — biggest disappointment source

customerExpectations.label: Short, practical application time
customerExpectations.reason: 5-15 min preferred over overnight; kids can't sit still

customerExpectations.label: Safe for kids from 6 months+
customerExpectations.reason: Non-irritating, no neurotoxins, no burning eyes

customerExpectations.label: Sufficient quantity for family/long hair
customerExpectations.reason: One 150ml bottle should treat multiple heads

customerExpectations.label: Easy to rinse / non-greasy
customerExpectations.reason: Users hate oily residue requiring 3 shampoos

reviews.t: Good product but arrived half unscrewed, all greasy packaging, not very happy. Luckily there's some left from my first delivery, but 20 euros for half a bottle is too expensive.

This is the second time I've used this product on my daughter's hair but it doesn't work! Lice are still alive even when spraying a lot and combing (the lice slip away and hide), and the nits don't die because a week later new lice appear. I reapply the same product and comb again, but it keeps happening... I tested it on recovered lice — the product absolutely doesn't kill them! We pay for ineffective products... Very disappointed.
Close expiration date and dented box. You shouldn't exaggerate! Just because we buy online doesn't mean we should get defective packages with close expiry dates!
I received the item and it expires in less than 2 months. It was manufactured in 2022. I wouldn't take the risk of putting it on my child's head!!!

Very disappointed. After the first shampoo, combing through, the lice were still alive and the following week tiny baby lice hatched, so the nits clearly weren't dead either... I repeated the shampoo and my long-haired daughters were losing hair by the handful 😱 and lice still not dead... So I switched to coconut oil overnight under cellophane every weekend and everything died along with daily combing — we managed to get out of it. Another expensive product with unkept promises.
Product pierced during transit because packaging too thin. Expiry date way too short — manufactured in 2023 so expires in 4 months. Product from Spain. Item returned.

Product that doesn't work, and I really don't understand the other positive comments. I tested this on my three kids and myself and lice and nits stay alive after application and several days later :-(. I don't recommend this product.

Didn't work on my daughter. I waited more than 30 minutes with the comb and managed to remove lice and nits but not dead ones. There are still many. I'm going to buy another product. For the price I'm very disappointed.
The box arrived open and dirty inside. The comb was dirty. It's shameful!!
Opened and expired products, written in Spanish.

I bought this to treat an early infestation, attracted by the '5 minutes' promise. ⏳ Big downside on shelf life: I received the bottle at the end of January with an expiration date barely 2 months away. For a product often kept 'just in case' in the medicine cabinet, that's really too short. Impossible to store for next back-to-school. 🤷‍♂️ Average efficacy: application is simple (spray + comb) but result wasn't radical in one go. Contrary to the '100% effective' promise, I found live lice af...
Very disappointed. Didn't work on us even though we scrupulously followed the instructions.

I've already used this very effective product. But today I received an imported product with French instructions stuck on the packaging, and above all a product at end-of-life to use within 6 months. Total validity of 3 years. OK it's a flash sale but this is really borderline.
Disappointed in the product: despite several applications, the lice are still there. The smell is strong and the pose time quite long. I expected a better result.

Didn't work at all on my daughters... I was excited by all those testimonials but apart from the product being easy to apply, the rest is zero... The lice are still alive and the nits too! I managed to remove a good portion with the comb but tomorrow I'll have to do another treatment...
It's not 100%. The comb and perseverance are also needed to kill all these bugs.
Little result for the very high price... I still have lice en masse... I'll switch to another brand.
The product has an effect, no doubt, but even for small-sized nits I needed at least three applications over several days. So '5 min flat' = bullshit.
Despite 2-3 shampoos there are still some left.
Done twice for my daughter and the whole family. Instructions followed to the letter but there were still some after. Forced to redo the treatment again.

Spray format — I tried applying it alone — carnage, I put it everywhere, it squirts everywhere. The texture isn't pleasant at all and I don't find it effective. Plus it's much more expensive than others. Very disappointed in this product 😁
Maybe it works... but I'm skeptical.
Received in a crushed box!
After several applications, the result is not conclusive — I'm going to change product.
Warning. Product arrived quickly but the comb wasn't included, even though the packaging clearly says 'comb included'... Not yet tested.
Not very effective and hurts the eyes. 5 min is far from enough.
Still lice the next day despite an hour of pose, using the balm and combing through... Plus it leaves hair extremely greasy and stains.

I'm not totally convinced. Given my daughter's thick hair I put quite a lot on. Removed the maximum with a comb (not the one provided — useless on curly hair). Check the next day, she still had a few live nits, and at day +3 still small lice. So I redid everything by hand and put product on again as prevention.

Ordered the first time, I received the cardboard packaging of the product without the product — the tape was open. Replacement of the product — on receipt, it had leaked, 3/4 of the product was empty... So rating 3/5 because I had to go to the pharmacy across town since they don't carry this brand everywhere. However the product is extremely effective, not irritating (a very important point for us with our atopic skin) and above all very fast to apply! Complete with the AS2000 comb and it's p...

Item received on time, the comb was present but dirty 🤔. I used it on my daughter who has an early infestation, on my other daughter and me as prevention. The format is very hard to use — the pump moves a lot in use, so you get it everywhere. I had nail polish on before using it... After treating 2 heads there wasn't much left on my nails (scary!). One of my daughters found it aggressive (apparently a burning sensation) and breathing it wasn't great either. After 5 min of pose I still saw liv...
Short expiry date (5 months vs >1.5 years at the pharmacy) so short-term use only...
Not powerful enough in a single application.
Not as good as claimed. After several treatments at respected intervals, still some nits.
Practical but not effective in the long run.

I bought it after reading the reviews — well I'm disappointed. Doesn't work the first time, even the second, even the third... Still those nits that are hard to exterminate... When will there be a truly effective product against nits, because they are the real problem 😅. It limits them but the product isn't magic either...

Really burns the eyes if it has the misfortune to run slightly during the pose. I had trouble tolerating it as an adult when it ran a bit during pose. Yet it's supposedly accessible to kids from 6 months... In terms of efficacy, 2 times is better than one.

Product effective against lice and nits, but one application isn't enough. Must be repeated at least 2 times to definitively get rid of lice... which means 150ml isn't enough to treat the whole family, especially with long hair. Combing is essential, but the included comb isn't suitable — plastic and rather fragile. Better to invest in a metal comb, more effective and durable. Ultimately the product is effective (with at least two treatments).

Effective on lice in a single application — indeed lice don't survive. As for nits, that's another story. The treatment can't be done just once because lice reappear (so the 'one application' promise isn't real), surely because of nits that resisted. The product is effective with a very short pose time, but you won't definitively get rid of lice in one application. Another downside: 150ml is a small quantity when you have long hair and a whole family to treat multiple times.

I recommend this product every time because it's very effective. It doesn't have the same smell or texture at all as the ones I tried before (which didn't work). That said, I have the impression it was very effective the first use (then my son caught lice again, so me too) and slightly less well on subsequent uses. The first time it was settled in one go (you really have to spread the product everywhere, right to the tips). Spray to spread over the whole head, insisting on the scalp and massa...
Product as described. The lotion format is more practical for use on kids aged 3 and 5.
Very effective, I only buy this one, but I find it a bit expensive (a real budget when you have kids in nursery school 😮‍💨😮‍💨).
Simple to use and effective.
Effective product but you have to comb well otherwise the nits don't come off.

My daughter in nursery school had recurring lice problems. We kept treating her during school holidays, but as soon as she went back to school it started again... Eventually not only did her scalp start getting patches but mostly the lice seemed to start resisting the reference product bought in parapharmacy. One application with this and — nothing left! However, the product arrived with the applicator broken. We didn't pay attention, used the product and temporarily placed it on a wooden bat...
Very effective and easy to put on hair.

Very easy to use.

The product is extremely effective. Careful though to protect your eyes. I had my eyes closed and didn't spray toward my eyes, yet the droplets in the air strongly irritated my eyes. The only solution was to pour three glasses of water onto my open eye. Makes me hesitate if I want to use it on my younger children... This incident led me to leave it on 10 min instead of 5 and my scalp remembers it! Despite everything, it's so effective that I give 4 stars.

For my daughter with very long, thick, straight hair, I did strand by strand as at the hairdresser, combing scrupulously with the provided comb. Product indeed very greasy but effective. Pose lasted about 15 min total combing time. You can see the lice dying on the comb. Nits are a bit trickier as they stay attached to the hair. You must persist and have patience. Then we did the Paranix extra-strong shampoo twice, leaving it on 5 min each time. I'm satisfied with this product.

OK it worked — let's see if the lice come back. Comb is poor quality as always. The product is like oil. ATTENTION ⚠️: you spray it everywhere and rub like a normal shampoo, but at the end the child must keep their head upright or tilted back — the product runs easily toward the eyes during pose. Have a clean towel ready to wipe up.
Good product for the first use but quickly becomes less useful on subsequent uses.

I used this anti-lice treatment because I noticed my daughter had nits in her hair. Application is really simple — just spray the lotion on the scalp and let it work for 5 minutes after massaging a bit for penetration. A comb is provided which makes removing dead lice easier. I only applied the product once and my daughter hasn't had lice problems since. So it's effective even if the price is quite high!
Good but expensive.
Product is top but comb isn't very good quality. The product is very effective but I had to redo it 10 days later because I think some nits had hatched.
Effective product but it literally burns the eyes! We didn't expect it — my son screamed.
Several treatments are needed to get rid of lice and nits.
It's effective. Better than Marie Rose or others. It removes all lice and nits. Nothing to say. Ready to rebuy if my daughter catches them again. Hope not 😊

Rather satisfied because I had another product that didn't work, and this one — first go, nothing left. Though the nits are dead but not detached.
